Selected Toolchain Project

E2E GUI XCP Bypassing App

Python / C++ / XCP / CAN-FD / Shared Memory / ONNX

Laptop-only real-vehicle AI bypass validation on a mass-production ADAS ECU.

The page keeps the clean resume format but restores the original website behavior: click blocks in the architecture diagrams to open detailed implementation modals.

Interactive Modals RestoredVehicle ECU BypassXCP DAQ/STIMCAN-FD 64B DTO C++ BackendPython RuntimeA2L / ELF Mapping

Objective

Commercial-vehicle ADAS needed a practical way to validate learned models in real vehicles without scarce passenger-car AI hardware. The platform uses the existing mass-production ADAS ECU, laptop inference, and real-time XCP communication.

Runtime Data Path
Vehicle ECUDAQ/STIM via XCP
VN1630AVector CAN-FD
C++ Backendxcp_backend.exe
Shared Memoryseqlock IPC
Python FrontendPyQt5 + pyqtgraph
AI RuntimeONNX or .py model

Original Main UI Screenshot

Simulink-like canvas for connecting signal, model, output, and XCP runtime blocks.

System Architecture — Click Blocks

Click ECU, DAQ, MTA/STIM, C++ backend, Python backend, UI, utility, and model blocks to show the detailed explanation below the diagram.

Shared Memory Layout — Click Blocks

Click shared-memory layout blocks to inspect IPC structure and signal-slot responsibilities.

Team Impact

  • Lets engineers test AI models on many commercial vehicles with a laptop and existing ADAS ECU.
  • Reduces dependence on scarce external AI hardware and CANape-only workflows.
  • Creates a bridge from rule-based ADAS logic toward learned-model validation in actual vehicles.
